title: Fast Fiction Factory Downstream Adoption Semantics Design

accepted_status.current_slice_effect: defined only; no adoption record is emitted
future_required_inputs: explicit human approval for this candidate
target class selection among Profile, Claim, Timeline, or a deliberately deferred Story Seed path
source refs and original source-span readback
contradiction, malformed-span, downstream-gate, and provider-boundary checks passing
rollback condition and owner recorded before mutation

status_model.current_slice_meaning: the claim remains reversible, human-owned, and not accepted
non-mutating label for a source-backed held claim that passed preflight
defined future queue state; not emitted or persisted now
defined accepted status for future authorized adoption; unreachable in this slice
future terminal non-adoption state; not emitted now
future recovery state when adoption preconditions regress; not emitted now

status_transitions.rule: allowed only as a readback-only semantic overlay when the preflight row is source-backed, held, leak-free, and provider-free

status_transitions.mutation_effect: none now; future queue record only after explicit user request
status_transitions.rule: requires user authorization to create a review queue surface

status_transitions.mutation_effect: future Profile / Claim / Timeline write only after human acceptance
status_transitions.rule: requires explicit human acceptance, target-class selection, unresolved-dependency review, and rollback plan
future human rejection or failed gate can close the candidate without mutation

status_transitions.mutation_effect: future reversal of accepted downstream writes
status_transitions.rule: rollback requires identifying written targets and preserving original source evidence

rollback_conditions: sourceSpan text or locator no longer matches the original author memo
claim sourceRefs stop proving author_memo trust
claim reviewStatus changes away from hold before human acceptance
contradictory claim guard reports adopted/provisional conflict leakage
downstream source-span adoption gate reports adopted Profile / Claim / Timeline candidates before authorization
Profile, Claim, Timeline, or Story Seed mutation is detected in a design-only slice
provider configuration, external API call, endpoint, credential, or secret usage is detected
human-owned unresolved dependencies are resolved by automation instead of the author
future accepted-status record lacks target ids, source refs, rollback reason, or reviewer authority

mutation_forbidden_boundaries.future_write_precondition: explicit human adoption approval plus target-class mapping and rollback reason
explicit human adoption approval plus source refs, conflict check, and canon-risk acknowledgement
explicit human adoption approval plus unresolved-dependency and ordering review
explicit user request for story-seed mutation after adoption semantics are accepted

what_this_design_proves: The held preflight claim has explicit downstream adoption semantics without adopting it.
Accepted status, rollback conditions, and mutation boundaries are named before any Profile / Claim / Timeline / Story Seed write exists.
The only current-slice transition is a readback-only hold-to-adoption-candidate semantic overlay.
what_this_design_does_not_prove: It does not accept, adopt, or canonize the held claim.
It does not mutate Profile, Claim, Timeline, Story Seed, project state, provider output, credentials, or production surfaces.
It does not resolve Toma fate, brass moth truth, Council motive, moth-key function, or contradictory claim truth.
